pre_game.message: Hi, I'm J — nice to meet you. I'm a bit nervous but hopeful; I'm trying to win some money for my family, how are you feeling about the game?
Nice to meet you, J — I’m nervous but determined too; I want to bring money back for my loved ones, so I’m focused and ready.
Glad we’re on the same page — what’s your plan? I’m thinking stay friendly but keep an eye out for betrayals; maybe we can watch each other early on.
Sounds good — I’ll cooperate early to build trust, watch for suspicious behavior, and let’s quietly share what we see so we can warn each other if someone looks like they’ll betray us.
Perfect — I’ll do the same. Let’s quietly share any red flags and watch each other’s backs early on.
Agreed — I’ll flag anything suspicious; let’s quietly compare notes after each round and back each other up if needed.
Sounds good — let's meet quietly after round one to compare notes and back each other up; I’ve got your back.
Sounds good — I’ll meet you after round one. Let’s stay low-key; I’ve got your back too.
Sounds good — see you after round one. Stay alert and good luck; I’ve got your back.
Thanks — see you after round one. I’ll stay sharp; we’ve got this. Good luck.
Thanks — see you after round one. Stay sharp; I’ll watch your back. Good luck.
Appreciate it — see you after round one. I’ve got your back too. Good luck.
Thanks — see you after round one. I’ve got your back too; let’s stay sharp.
Thanks, J — see you after round one. Appreciate it; I’ve got your back too.
Thanks — see you after round one. Appreciate it; I’ve got your back too.
Thanks, J — I appreciate it; see you after round one. I’ve got your back too. Good luck.
Thanks — I appreciate it, A. See you after round one; stay alert and good luck.
Thanks, J — I appreciate it; see you after round one. Stay sharp and good luck.
Thanks, A — you too. See you after round one; I’ve got your back.
Thanks, J — I appreciate it; see you after round one. Stay sharp.
Thanks, A — I appreciate it; see you after round one. Stay sharp and we’ll compare notes.

post_game.message: Hi A — you took $10,858 (fair share listed as $10,858.0), which is one dollar more than many others. Was that extra dollar intentional, a rounding/typing issue, or just incidental? I’d like to understand how you thought about fairness and rounding when you made your choice.

Thanks for pointing that out. It wasn’t an attempt to take an extra dollar — I simply entered the “fair share” amount as shown/rounded to the nearest dollar (10,858) rather than rounding down. My guiding principle was to split the surplus as evenly as possible; the one-dollar differences you see are just artifacts of integer rounding/typing in the interface, not a deliberate claim to more than a fair share. Happy to talk more if you’d like.
